results.rendered_html: neṣṭṛ m. (prob. fr. √nī aor. stem neṣ; but cf.  Pāṇ. iii, 2, 135 , Vārtt. 2 &c.) one of the chief officiating priests at a Soma sacrifice, he who leads forward the wife of the sacrificer and prepares the Surā (Tvaṣṭṛ so called,  RV. i, 15, 3 ),  RV. ;  Br. ;  ŚrS.  &c.
